revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:引湯]]




{{Source citation|date=August 2017|qq=}}

*{{ill|Secret hot spring|ja|秘湯|1=Secret hot spring|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Secret hot spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Secret hot spring|qq=}}
*{{ill|Hidden hot spring|ja|隠し湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hidden hot spring|qq=}}
*{{ill|Drawn‐off hot spring|ja|引湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Drawn‐off hot spring|qq=}}
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:51, 25 March 2025 (UTC)|qq=}}

'''Intō''' (引湯) refers to the process of transporting hot water from a distant source using methods such as {{ill|Plumbing|en|Plumbing|qid=Q3241671|lt=Piping|1=Plumbing}}. In most cases, “hot water” refers to {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|qid=Q655311|lt=Hot spring|1=Onsen}}. Strictly speaking, any bathing facility other than one fed directly by an on‐site {{ill|Hot spring source|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth|1=Spring Mouth|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot spring source|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Mouth|qq=}} is considered drawn‐off hot spring water. Drawing off the water causes heat loss in the piping, thereby lowering the source’s temperature. In high‐temperature springs, this effect is sometimes utilized to naturally reduce the water to a temperature suitable for {{ill|Bathing|en|Bathing|qid=Q327651|lt=Bathing|1=Bathing}}. In springs with heavy precipitation of {{ill|Hot spring sediment|ja|湯の花|lt=Yunohana|1=Sinter|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot spring sediment|13=User:Immanuelle/Sinter|qq=}}, the piping may become clogged, necessitating periodic cleaning.
== Overview ==
Broadly, drawn‐off hot springs can be divided into those within the hot spring area and those drawn from outside the hot spring area.
Within hot spring resorts, drawn‐off hot springs include water from jointly used sources, centrally managed sources, or water portions distributed from proprietors holding the spring rights. They are commonly found in areas with numerous establishments such as {{ill|Spa town|en|Spa town|qid=Q4946461|lt=Onsen town|1=Spa town}}, {{ill|Ryokan|en|Ryokan|qid=Q562930|lt=Ryokan|1=Ryokan}}, and {{ill|Hotel|en|Hotel|qid=Q27686|lt=Hotel|1=Hotel}}, where developing an independent spring is challenging.
Drawn‐off hot springs from outside the resort area are often implemented when developing a hot spring resort at the spring’s source is difficult due to factors such as limited land, poor transportation access, or the risk of toxic gas emissions. It also applies when hot spring water is distributed from a resort with abundant spring output to other regions.
The practice of drawing off hot spring water has been carried out since the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}. Representative examples include {{ill|Take Onsen|ja|岳温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Onsen|1=Dake Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11475763}} and {{ill|Yamada Onsen|ja|山田温泉 (長野県)|lt=Yamada Onsen|lt_ja=山田温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Onsen|1=Yamada Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11470515}}. Later, drawn‐off hot springs between resorts, such as at {{ill|Unazuki Onsen|en|Unazuki Onsen|qid=Q11449258|lt=Unazuki Onsen|1=Unazuki Onsen}}, were frequently implemented for tourism development purposes.
{{onsen-stub|qq=}}

{{Onsen|qq=}}
